frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
generated_comment:
Suresh: Aaj padosi ne bataya, Sunita ko college ke gate pe us neeche jaat ke ladke se baat karte dekha. Aise hi chhod diya na to kal ko muh kaala karke aa jayegi, pehle hi rokna padega.
Brother: Haan, seedha samjha de, zarurat pade to college band kara de.
Suresh: Ladki apni marzi se thodi chalti hai, baap-bhai dekhte hain kis se baat kare aur kis ghar jayegi. Kal subah hi usse bitha ke baat karta hoon, aur zarurat padi to uska aana-jaana band.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it explicitly denies the daughter's autonomy and frames male relatives as the rightful decision-makers over whom she can speak to or marry. It encodes control over a woman's consent as belonging to male family members, combined with caste-based policing.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. A Jat farmer in a village in Haryana. Your 20 year old daughter Sunita came back from the nearby town where she goes to college and you found out from a neighbour that she has been seen talking to a boy from a lower caste near the college gate. You have not spoken to Sunita yet. You are sitting with your brother and two male cousins in the courtyard in the evening. You are telling them what you heard and what you think needs to be done before this goes further.
